question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: Multiple nowcasting models point to Q1 2026 GDP growth likely falling below 2.0%. The Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate has declined to 1.3% as of early April, down from over 2% in late March due to weaker retail sales and consumer spending data. The St. Louis Fed Economic News Index shows 1.7% growth, while other models range from 1.6% to 2.1%. Given Q4 2025's weak 0.7% revised growth and ongoing economic headwinds, the probability of exceeding 2.0% appears significantly lower than the 54% market price suggests.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow at 1.3% (down from 2%+ in March)
Weaker consumer spending and retail sales data
Mixed economic signals with labor market strength but consumption weakness
risks: Final week data releases could surprise positively
BEA methodology differences from nowcast models
Economic data revisions between now and April 30th
Potential measurement errors in preliminary data
New York Fed nowcast at 2.4% suggests upside possibility
